response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with strong breadth across JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, but the role requires deep Java/Kotlin/JVM expertise and Spring ecosystem experience, which are essentially absent from the resume. Java appears only as a listed skill with minimal context (robotic arms, mobile orchestration tooling years ago), and there is no Kotlin, Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or IDE-internals experience evidenced. The closest IDE-adjacent work is C/C++ on LabWindows/CVI a decade ago. Mismatch on the core required qualifications is too large to justify an interview for a mid-senior JVM tooling role.
strengths: Long track record on complex, large-scale systems and developer tooling
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development
Some IDE-adjacent C/C++ experience on LabWindows/CVI at National Instruments
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin experience and only marginal Java exposure
No Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate ecosystem experience shown
No evidence of JVM internals or modern IDE/language-tooling work

key_factors.factor: Minimal Java and no Kotlin experience
Solid engineering craftsmanship and complex-systems track record
